# TESS Program G08146

Title: Searching For Long-Period Post Common Envelope Binaries In The Extended TESS Sectors

PI: Wilson, David - University Of Colorado At Boulder

Type: SMALL

Summary: Many stellar binary systems undergo a common envelope evolution, producing astrophysically interesting systems such as cataclysmic variables and novae. The exact common envelope process is poorly understood, with theoretical work relying on the period distribution of detached post common envelope binaries (PCEBs). The period distribution has a gap produced by detection limits which the extended Sectors of TESS Cycle 8 are poised to fill. By observing dozens of systems in the extended sectors we can search for weeks-long periods, completing the period distribution of PCEBs and shedding light on this key aspect of stellar evolution.


# Sector 097 targets observed (2 min: 133, 20 s: 1)
